statusLine._doc: v7.0.0-alpha (2026-05-22) — executive view of the active SDD pipeline run. Reads workspace/output/db/console.db (table runs / events) and emits [AGENT] X% · FEAT N-Name · US m-Name · elapsed. Cheap (single SELECT, < 30 ms, no LLM). Fallback `[SDD] no run` when console.db absent. Disable per-dev via .claude/settings.local.json overriding `statusLine` to null. Detail: .claude/python/sdd_admin/statusline.py docstring.
statusLine.command: python -c "import os,sys,pathlib; r=os.environ.get('CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR') or next((str(p) for p in [pathlib.Path.cwd()]+list(pathlib.Path.cwd().parents) if (p/'.claude').is_dir()),'.'); sys.path.insert(0,r+'/.claude/python'); from sdd_admin import statusline; statusline.main()"

permissions._allow_bash_doc: v7.0.0-alpha security hardening 2026-06-06 — `Bash(*)` catch-all retiré (fail-open). Toute invocation Bash hors allowlist explicite déclenche un prompt utilisateur (defaultMode=default). Pour ajouter une commande légitime : éditer cette liste OU settings.local.json (per-dev, gitignored). Deny rules ci-dessous restent en place comme filet de défense additionnel.
permissions._deny_bypass_doc: v7.0.0-alpha (audit 2026-06-05) — defense-in-depth contre les bypass envvar SDD_ALLOW_* / SDD_DISABLE_*. Les hooks Python lisaient déjà ces variables et les audit-loggaient, mais rien n'empêchait un agent (ou un Bash exécutif) de les exporter en cours de session. Ces deny rules bloquent les patterns d'export les plus courants (POSIX export, inline VAR=val, PowerShell $env:, Windows setx). Bypass volontaire Tech Lead : éditer settings.local.json (gitignored) et retirer la deny pour la session.
permissions._deny_obfuscation_doc: v7.0.0-alpha (audit C4-hardening 2026-06-06) — defense-in-depth contre vecteurs d'obfuscation classiques : (a) encoded PowerShell (-EncodedCommand bypass string matchers), (b) IEX/Invoke-Expression download-execute, (c) base64 decoding inline, (d) downloaders alternatifs (certutil, bitsadmin) qui esquivent curl/wget deny, (e) reverse shells (nc -e, /dev/tcp), (f) persistence (reg add HKLM, scheduled tasks, authorized_keys append), (g) elevation (net user /add). Compatible un workflow dev légitime — aucune de ces commandes n'est utilisée par le pipeline SDD.
permissions._defaultMode_doc: v7.0.0-alpha (2026-05-21) : 'acceptEdits' (auto-accept Edit/Write/NotebookEdit silently) → 'default' (prompts on tools not in allow). Edit/Write/MultiEdit are explicitly allowlisted above so the SDD pipeline still flows ; this defaultMode change blocks ONLY tools that slip outside the curated allowlist. To restore the legacy auto-accept behaviour (faster but less safe), set `defaultMode: 'acceptEdits'` in .claude/settings.local.json (per-dev, gitignored).
permissions._deny_M9_doc: Audit 2026-06-06 M9 — defense-in-depth additions : (a) shell-nesting HTTP fetch (bash -c, sh -c, powershell -c, cmd /c) to close the gap where curl/wget could escape Bash matcher via inline shell; (b) critical user-data paths under workspace/input/ and workspace/output/{us,src,plans} not covered by the broad rm -rf workspace/output/* allow pattern.

SessionStart._doc: v7.0.0+ (2026-06-08, emprunt superpowers v5.1) — session_start injecte le banner SDDPro au démarrage (startup|resume|clear|compact). Texte statique + 1 Glob workspace/input/feats/ = ~5 ms, 0 token LLM. Fournit `additionalContext` qui prime Claude Code sur le pipeline canonique, les 13 commandes user-facing et l'état détecté du projet. Mode CI (env CI=1) → version minimale 1L. Bypass complet : SDD_DISABLE_SESSION_START=1.

hooks.command: python -c "import os,sys,pathlib; r=os.environ.get('CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR') or next((str(p) for p in [pathlib.Path.cwd()]+list(pathlib.Path.cwd().parents) if (p/'.claude').is_dir()),'.'); sys.path.insert(0,r+'/.claude/python'); import _hook; _hook.run('sdd_hooks.session_start')"

PreToolUse._doc: v7.0.0-alpha (audit P0-security 2026-06-05) — block_env_bypass closes the gap left by case-sensitive Bash deny patterns. Receives the Bash command on stdin, refuses any case-insensitive attempt to SET a SDD_ALLOW_* or SDD_DISABLE_* envvar mid-session (export, NAME=val inline, $env:, setx, Set-Variable). Companion to the `_deny_bypass_doc` patterns in permissions.deny.

hooks.command: python -c "import os,sys,pathlib; r=os.environ.get('CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR') or next((str(p) for p in [pathlib.Path.cwd()]+list(pathlib.Path.cwd().parents) if (p/'.claude').is_dir()),'.'); sys.path.insert(0,r+'/.claude/python'); import _hook; _hook.run('sdd_hooks.block_env_bypass')"

hooks._doc: v7.0.0-alpha Sprint 1.4 (2026-06-06) — pre_write_lint enforces stack-CLAUDE.md forbidden patterns (Kotlin !!, Vue raw HTML, console.log, hex hardcode, etc.) at GENERATION time. Default WARN-only (exit 0 + audit log); SDD_PRE_WRITE_LINT_STRICT=1 to make exit 2 (blocking). Bypass via SDD_DISABLE_PRE_WRITE_LINT=1. Skips test paths and non-src files.

hooks._doc: v7.0.0+ (audit P3 TDD 2026-06-08, emprunt Superpowers v5.1 test-driven-development) — enforce_tdd détecte écriture de NOUVEAU symbole production (function/class/method/endpoint) sous workspace/output/src/ SANS test compagnon. Default `warn` interactif, `strict` en CI. Bypass : SDD_DISABLE_TDD=1 (one-shot, audit-loggué) OU SDD_TDD_MODE=off. Reconnaît .NET (.cs)/Node-TS/Python/Kotlin/Java. Refactor / Edit existing code = allowed.

hooks._doc: v7.0.0+ (audit P3 B + B.bis 2026-06-08) — enforce_two_stage_auditor blocks Stage B reviewers (code/security/arch) from spawning before Stage A (spec-compliance-reviewer) has produced a fresh non-RED verdict in qa_spec_compliance. Default mode `AuditorBatchMode=two-stage` ; bypass `AuditorBatchMode: legacy-parallel` (config) or `SDD_BYPASS_TWO_STAGE=1` (one-shot env). Idempotent : pure DB read, no side effects.

PreToolUse._doc: v7.0.0-alpha (audit C5, 2026-06-05) — validate_stack_combo before /sdd-full, /sdd-poc, /dev-run skills. Closes the gap 'script existed but never wired'. Non-blocking on experimental combos (exit 1 → WARN only). Blocks on untested (exit 2) and invalid (exit 3) unless SDD_ALLOW_UNTESTED_COMBO=1.

hooks._doc: v7.0.0+ (audit P3 A + A.bis 2026-06-08) — auto_invoke_complexity_router runs the deterministic Python complexity_router.py BEFORE /sdd-full /sdd-poc /dev-run when `ComplexityRouterMode: auto` is set in Project Config. A.bis idempotent guard skips subprocess if a fresh report (< 1h) already exists. Never blocks — additive only. Bypass: SDD_DISABLE_AUTO_ROUTER=1 OR ComplexityRouterMode!=auto.

PreToolUse._doc: v7.0.0-alpha (audit CTO 2026-06-07) — preflight_glob_scope blocks unbounded Globs (workspace/output/src/**/* without extension). Defense-in-depth against the token explosion post-mortem documented in agents/spec-compliance-reviewer.md (1.8M tokens / $35 from one bad Glob). Default WARN ; strict via SDD_GLOB_SCOPE_STRICT=1 ; bypass via SDD_DISABLE_GLOB_SCOPE=1.

PostToolUse._doc: v7.0.0-alpha (audit P2, 2026-06-05) — validate_stack_consistency enforces 1 backend XOR 1 fullstack OR 1 mobile, et 1 frontend SPA max. Filtre interne aux édits de workspace/input/stack/stack.md, exit 2 + [STACK_MULTI_INCOHERENT] sur violation. Bypass via SDD_ALLOW_MULTISTACK=1 (bench/debug).

PostToolUse._doc: v7.0.0-alpha (2026-06-04, audit CRIT-5) — validate_augment_contract enforces preserves:/adds: contracts from plan v2 augment blocks. The hook skips silently on Write (file creation), on test files, and on edits outside workspace/output/src/. Exit 2 + [PRESERVES_VIOLATED]/[ADDS_VIOLATED] on violation. Documented as PostToolUse Edit|Write|MultiEdit in .claude/python/README.md and .claude/docs/hooks-and-protections.md §1.3 — previously orphan (defined + tested but never wired).

SubagentStop._doc: v7.0.0-alpha (audit P0-workflow 2026-06-05) — resolve_po_hash_sentinel auto-resolves `Parent FEAT hash: sha256:COMPUTE_REQUIRED` in US files whenever the `po` agent stops, regardless of invocation path. Previously the sentinel was resolved ONLY by /us-generate STEP 3.0; standalone `Agent: po` invocations left the sentinel in place, causing all downstream agents to emit [FEAT_HASH_MISMATCH]. The hook is non-blocking (defense-in-depth safety net).
